steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This Cross-Text Connections question asks how Text 1 and Text 2 relate. The decisive clues: Text 1 reports Wang et al.'s surprising eDNA finding and then presents Miller and Simpson's hypothesis to explain it. Text 2 identifies a fatal flaw in that hypothesis — surface remains would be destroyed by scavengers and weathering in under a thousand years, far less than the several millennia the hypothesis requires.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Text 1 discusses two approaches without advocating, Text 2 advocates for one: Text 1 does not weigh two competing research approaches; it presents one study's findings and one explanatory hypothesis. Text 2 does not advocate for an approach at all — it refutes the hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice B — Text 1 presents findings and another team's explanation; Text 2 provides detail that calls that explanation into question: Matches exactly: Text 1 = Wang et al.'s eDNA findings + Miller/Simpson preservation hypothesis; Text 2 = scavengers and weathering undermine the preservation hypothesis. ✓
  • Choice C — Text 1 describes the study and a critique of its methodology; Text 2 defends that methodology: Text 1 contains no methodological critique; it presents a positive hypothesis. Text 2 does not defend Wang et al.'s methods; it attacks the Miller/Simpson hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice D — Text 1 argues new research undermines the standard view; Text 2 suggests reconciling the two: Both the "undermining" finding and the attempted reconciliation appear in Text 1. Text 2 does the opposite of reconciling — it shows the reconciliation attempt fails. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
